preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar lateral affricate [tɬ] to the voiceless velar lateral affricate [kʟ] is a process of retraction. This backing of the place of articulation is observed in languages with complex lateral series where dorsality is a primary distinctive feature.

examples.note: Retraction of lateral affricates in specific environments or dialectal variants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
